d1_emptied_before.text: Grandpa always said: start with what the room told everyone and nobody heard. The whole district saw that vault. What else did the vault show them?
Grandpa always said: locks are witnesses too. Ida's lock had fifty years to learn how to complain, and tonight it didn't.
The lock, Gibbons. Fifty years of Ida's care, and tonight — not one complaint. Sit with what that means.

d3_theft_window.text: Grandpa always said: when a story feels too big, find the witness who counted. Somebody in this town counts everything.
The checklist, Gibbons. Laminated, time-stamped, three nights early. Read what it proves — not about her. About the calendar.

d5_lodger.text: Grandpa always said: a photograph is a witness that never blinks. Yours caught something the district rolls say doesn't exist.
The tail, Gibbons. Fifty years of records without a weasel — and Margie's inn took in somebody new last week. Read her gossip again.

d6_insider.text: Grandpa always said: when a witness gives you one strange detail, that detail is the story. Ferris gave you a key.
Your very first deduction, Gibbons — the one from the ceremony night. It's been waiting on the board for a partner all week.

d7_signet.text: Grandpa always said: every mark belongs to a hand. You drew the mark days ago — now find the person who reads marks for a living.
The sketch, Gibbons. It's been pinned there since Day 2, waiting for an expert's eyes to finish it.

d13_final.text: Grandpa always said: the last step of a story is never a new fact. It's four old ones agreeing.
Motive. Means. Opportunity. Proof. The ledger row is waiting for all four deduction cards on one string.
The four gold cards, Gibbons. Every one of them earned on the record. Tie them, and then go be kind about it.

poppy_opened_not_emptied.text: Grandpa always said: re-read the witness who was most embarrassed. Embarrassment and guilt wear different coats.
The backwards key. A thief wipes her tracks; a worrier makes a list of them. Which kind hung that key back wrong?
